passages.title: Remembering people's names
passages.text:
Most of us have suffered the (0) EMBARRASSMENT of forgetting someone's name.
Often we fail to pay attention when (17) ___ are made, but later on in the conversation we don't want to appear (18) ___ by asking them what they're called.

Fortunately, there are some simple ways you can (19) ___ this problem. One is to improve your powers of (20) ___.

Practise studying faces in public places, making a mental note of physical (21) ___ such as high foreheads or narrow eyebrows. You'll be surprised what a wide (22) ___ of shapes and sizes people's features have.

Then, when you first meet someone, remember them as 'Laura with the small nose', for example. With surnames, make (23) ___ associations.

Finally, ending with the person's name, as in 'See you later, Max.' is a good way of (24) ___ that you don't forget it.

example.explanation: Ví dụ: sau 'the' cần danh từ. EMBARRASS (v) -> EMBARRASSMENT (n) = sự ngượng ngùng/bối rối.

questions.explanation: Sau 'when' cần danh từ chỉ sự kiện; thêm -tion và -s để thành danh từ số nhiều vì có thể có nhiều lần giới thiệu. INTRODUCE (v) -> INTRODUCTION (n) -> INTRODUCTIONS (n, plural).
Sau 'appear' cần tính từ; ngữ nghĩa là 'không lịch sự' khi hỏi lại tên, nên dùng phủ định. POLITE (adj) -> IMPOLITE (adj) với tiền tố im-.
Cần động từ sau 'can' để diễn tả 'vượt qua' vấn đề; collocation tự nhiên là overcome a problem. COME (v) -> OVERCOME (v) (thêm over-).
Sau 'powers of' cần danh từ; OBSERVE (v) -> OBSERVATION (n). Đây là 'khả năng quan sát'.
Sau 'physical' cần danh từ; có ví dụ 'such as...' nên dùng số nhiều. CHARACTER (n) -> CHARACTERISTIC (n) -> CHARACTERISTICS (n, plural).
Cấu trúc 'a wide variety of' là collocation cố định. VARY (v) -> VARIETY (n) (đổi y -> i + -ety).
Sau 'make' cần tính từ bổ nghĩa cho 'associations' (liên tưởng bằng hình ảnh). VISION (n) -> VISUAL (adj).
Sau giới từ 'of' cần V-ing. ENSURE (v) = đảm bảo, phù hợp nghĩa 'một cách để đảm bảo bạn không quên'. SURE -> ENSURE (thêm en-) -> ENSURING (V-ing).

passages.title: Too many emails
passages.text:
Some years ago, there were (0) PREDICTIONS that email would soon be replaced as the most common means of online communication by social (17) ___ sites.

Since then, however, the number of emails sent has increased (18) ___, to the point that the volume received on a daily basis has become (19) ___ for many people.

For some users, the situation has become so (20) ___ that they regularly delete all the emails they receive without even bothering to read them, which is rather (21) ___, to say the least.

A less extreme measure is to install software that sorts incoming mail into different categories. By doing this, promotions from (22) ___ organisations go into a separate folder, (23) ___ us to see which emails are more important than others.

Not all of us, though, may be so keen to let software make such (24) ___ for us.

example.explanation: Ví dụ: sau 'there were' cần danh từ số nhiều. PREDICT (v) -> PREDICTION (n) -> PREDICTIONS (n, plural) = những dự đoán.

questions.explanation: Cụm đúng: social NETWORK sites / social NETWORKING sites (đều đúng). Sai thường gặp: NETWORKS/NETWORKED/NETWORKER vì sai loại từ hoặc không hợp cấu trúc 'social ___ sites'.
Cần trạng từ bổ nghĩa cho 'has increased'. STEADY (adj) -> STEADILY (adv) (đổi y -> i + -ly).
Sau 'has become' cần tính từ; nghĩa là 'gây căng thẳng'. STRESS (n/v) -> STRESSFUL (adj).
Cấu trúc 'so + adj + that...' cần tính từ; nghĩa là 'không thể chịu nổi' nên dùng phủ định. BEAR (v) -> BEARABLE (adj) -> UNBEARABLE (adj).
Sau 'rather' cần tính từ; nghĩa là 'rủi ro'. RISK (n/v) -> RISKY (adj) với hậu tố -y.
Cần tính từ bổ nghĩa cho 'organisations'. COMMERCE (n) -> COMMERCIAL (adj) (thường bỏ -e + -ial).
Sau dấu phẩy diễn tả kết quả, dùng V-ing; nghĩa là 'giúp/cho phép'. ABLE -> ENABLE (v) (thêm en-) -> ENABLING (V-ing).
Sau 'such' cần danh từ; thường là số nhiều vì 'such decisions'. DECIDE (v) -> DECISION (n) -> DECISIONS (n, plural).

passages.title: Advertising in schools
passages.text:
In certain countries, there has been rapid (0) GROWTH in the spending power of teenagers. Realising this, firms are aiming even more of their (17) ___ at young people, and (18) ___ they are doing this by advertising directly in schools.

These companies see children as the (19) ___ of the future, and hope that when they become adults they will maintain their (20) ___ to brands they first started buying in their youth.

As a result, many (21) ___ are keen to supply equipment to schools.

Other firms install machines selling snacks and drinks, and these are often highly (22) ___ for both the companies and the schools.

There is, however, considerable (23) ___ about whether this is a good idea. Some argue that these machines encourage (24) ___ eating habits.

example.explanation: Ví dụ: sau 'rapid' cần danh từ. GROW (v) -> GROWTH (n) = sự tăng trưởng.

questions.explanation: Sau 'their' cần danh từ; 'aim their products at young people' là collocation. PRODUCE (v) -> PRODUCT (n) -> PRODUCTS (n, plural).
Cần trạng từ bổ nghĩa cho cả mệnh đề (mức độ ngày càng). INCREASE (v/n) -> INCREASING (adj/participle) -> INCREASINGLY (adv).
Sau 'the' cần danh từ chỉ người; nghĩa là 'người tiêu dùng'. CONSUME (v) -> CONSUMER (n) -> CONSUMERS (n, plural).
Sau 'their' cần danh từ; collocation 'loyalty to brands'. LOYAL (adj) -> LOYALTY (n).
Cần danh từ chỉ người/công ty sản xuất; theo ngữ cảnh là nhiều hãng. MANUFACTURE (v) -> MANUFACTURER (n) -> MANUFACTURERS (n, plural).
Sau 'highly' cần tính từ; collocation 'highly profitable'. PROFIT (n/v) -> PROFITABLE (adj).
Sau 'considerable' cần danh từ; nghĩa là 'bất đồng' về việc đó có tốt không. AGREE -> DISAGREE (v) -> DISAGREEMENT (n).
Cần tính từ bổ nghĩa 'eating habits'; nghĩa là 'không lành mạnh' vì máy bán snack/đồ uống ngọt. HEALTH (n) -> HEALTHY (adj) -> UNHEALTHY (adj).

passages.title: Preparing for an interview
passages.text:
Before you go to an interview, think about your (0) APPEARANCE.
When you make your (17) ___ of clothes, avoid anything too casual but don't go to the other extreme of wearing items that are so formal you feel (18) ___ in them.

Make sure you know the exact (19) ___ of the building where the interview will take place. There's nothing worse than (20) ___ trying to find the right place as the time for it to begin rapidly approaches.

Once you reach the firm's offices, remember that the interview starts there. You never know who you might meet in lifts, corridors or waiting rooms, so try to make a good (21) ___ on everyone.

When you go into the interview room, show you have (22) ___ in yourself, and strike an appropriate balance between formality and (23) ___.

Bear in mind that people tend to make a (24) ___ about someone they have met within a few minutes.

example.explanation: Ví dụ: sau 'your' cần danh từ. APPEAR (v) -> APPEARANCE (n) = ngoại hình/vẻ bề ngoài.

questions.explanation: Cụm cố định 'make your choice'. Sau 'make your' cần danh từ. CHOOSE (v) -> CHOICE (n).
Sau 'feel' cần tính từ; nghĩa là 'không thoải mái' nên dùng phủ định. COMFORT (n/v) -> COMFORTABLE (adj) -> UNCOMFORTABLE (adj).
Sau 'exact' cần danh từ; LOCATE (v) -> LOCATION (n).
Cần trạng từ bổ nghĩa cho 'trying'. DESPAIR (n/v) -> DESPERATE (adj) -> DESPERATELY (adv).
Collocation: 'make a good impression'. IMPRESS (v) -> IMPRESSION (n).
Sau 'have' cần danh từ. CONFIDENT (adj) -> CONFIDENCE (n).
Song song với 'formality' là danh từ; FRIEND (n) -> FRIENDLY (adj) -> FRIENDLINESS (n, -liness).
Sau 'make a' cần danh từ: make a JUDGEMENT/JUDGMENT (cả 2 chính tả đều chuẩn). Sai thường gặp: JUDGE/JUDGING/JUDGES vì không phải danh từ phù hợp trong cụm cố định.

passages.title: Jobs in the future
passages.text:
In twenty years' time, which jobs will people still be doing, and which is it (0) REASONABLE to assume will have gone forever?
Today's young people should give that question careful (17) ___ before choosing a career.

Clearly there will continue to be a need for staff in (18) ___ professions such as medicine that involve caring for others, and also for people able to persuade, like (19) ___ and lawyers.

There will of course be no (20) ___ of jobs in science and technology, particularly for individuals able to (21) ___ in newer fields such as biotechnology and microbiology.

On the other hand, the already rapid (22) ___ of jobs to robots will speed up even further, as they replace workers not only in manufacturing but also in the (23) ___ industry.

And as robots learn how to stack shelves, take over at check-outs or send us our online shopping, it won't be long before most supermarket jobs have (24) ___, too.

example.explanation: Ví dụ: sau 'is it' cần tính từ. REASON (n) -> REASONABLE (adj) = hợp lý.

questions.explanation: Cụm 'give careful consideration'. Sau 'give ... careful' cần danh từ. CONSIDER (v) -> CONSIDERATION (n).
Cần tính từ bổ nghĩa 'professions'. EXIST (v) -> EXISTING (adj) = hiện có.
Cần danh từ chỉ người, số nhiều (nhóm nghề). POLITICS (n) -> POLITICIAN (n) -> POLITICIANS (n, plural).
Collocation: 'no shortage of'. SHORT (adj) -> SHORTAGE (n) = sự thiếu hụt.
Sau 'able to' cần động từ nguyên mẫu: (to) SPECIALISE/SPECIALIZE (chấp nhận UK/US spelling). Sai thường gặp: SPECIALIST/SPECIALIZATION/SPECIALITY vì chuyển sang danh từ hoặc sai dạng.
Sau 'rapid' cần danh từ. LOSE (v) -> LOSS (n).
Cụm 'the construction industry' cần danh từ. CONSTRUCT (v) -> CONSTRUCTION (n).
Sau 'have' cần past participle (hiện tại hoàn thành). APPEAR (v) -> DISAPPEAR (v) -> DISAPPEARED (V3).

passages.title: Letting the sea in
passages.text:
The small (0) COASTAL town of Medmerry has found an unusual way to reduce the risk of flooding: let the sea in.

The scheme involved the (17) ___ of part of the existing sea wall and the building of seven kilometres of new, higher (18) ___ further inland, closer to local communities.

This has led to the (19) ___ of a large area of wetland, capable of absorbing the energy of the waves and therefore bringing about a (20) ___ reduction in flooding.

According to environmental scientists, this (21) ___ project will also turn the whole area into a (22) ___ nature reserve.

It is already attracting large numbers of birds and other wildlife and eventually it should provide a safe home for some of the country's most (23) ___ species.

This is bound to attract more visitors to the area, although it remains (24) ___ whether numbers will have to be limited.

example.explanation: Ví dụ: cần tính từ bổ nghĩa 'town'. COAST (n) -> COASTAL (adj) = ven biển.

questions.explanation: Sau 'the' cần danh từ. DESTROY (v) -> DESTRUCTION (n).
Cấu trúc: the building of ... higher DEFENCES/DEFENSES (số nhiều vì có 'seven kilometres of new, higher ...'). Sai thường gặp: DEFENCE/DEFENSE (số ít) hoặc DEFENDING (V-ing sai loại từ).
Sau 'led to the' cần danh từ. CREATE (v) -> CREATION (n).
Cần tính từ bổ nghĩa 'reduction'. SUBSTANCE (n) -> SUBSTANTIAL (adj) = đáng kể.
Cần tính từ bổ nghĩa 'project'. AMBITION (n) -> AMBITIOUS (adj).
Cần tính từ bổ nghĩa 'nature reserve'; nghĩa là 'rất lớn'. MASS (n) -> MASSIVE (adj).
Cần tính từ mô tả 'species'; collocation 'endangered species'. DANGER (n) -> ENDANGER (v) -> ENDANGERED (adj).
Sau 'remains' cần tính từ; nghĩa là 'chưa rõ' nên dùng phủ định. CLEAR (adj) -> UNCLEAR (adj) với tiền tố un-.

passages.title: My 16th birthday
passages.text: I turned 16 last week. And I have to say that (0) INITIALLY I didn't enjoy my birthday much. I usually get some birthday (17) ___ from my friends on social media. But nobody wished me a happy birthday, which was a bit (18) ___. After school, I noticed that the friends I usually walk home with had all (19) ___, so I walked back alone. But then when I got back to my house, there were lots of (20) ___ and balloons on the outside. 'OK,' I thought, 'this looks more (21) ___ inside, and there, waiting in the sitting room, were all of my school friends! Seeing them there was so (22) ___...! Together with my parents, they'd arranged this surprise party. And they'd done it all without my (23) ___! In fact, it was probably the most special and (24) ___ party ever!

example.explanation: Cần trạng từ bổ nghĩa cho cả câu.

questions.explanation: Cần danh từ số nhiều (những lời chúc). GREET (v) -> GREETINGS (n).
Cần tính từ chỉ tính chất của sự việc (việc không ai chúc mừng). DEPRESS -> DEPRESSING.
Ngữ cảnh là bạn bè đã biến mất/đi hết. APPEAR -> DISAPPEARED (thêm tiền tố phủ định và chia V3 sau 'had').
Cần danh từ số nhiều (nhiều đồ trang trí) đi sau 'lots of'. DECORATE -> DECORATIONS.
Cần tính từ sau 'looks'. PROMISE -> PROMISING (có vẻ hứa hẹn, khả quan).
Cần tính từ miêu tả sự bất ngờ. EXPECT -> UNEXPECTED (bất ngờ, không lường trước).
Cần danh từ sau tính từ sở hữu 'my'. KNOW -> KNOWLEDGE (sự hiểu biết / without my knowledge = mà tôi không hề hay biết).
Cần tính từ đứng trước danh từ 'party'. MEMORY -> MEMORABLE (đáng nhớ).

passages.title: Tetris: the most popular video game ever
passages.text: There is an almost (0) UNLIMITED number of video games on the market today and you could be forgiven for being under the (17) ___ that the best ones are those packed with special effects, requiring great technique. Nothing could be further from the (18) ___. The favourite game of all time is also one of the oldest and most basic: Tetris. The player has to fit a (19) ___ of shapes together on the screen. When a whole row is made without gaps between the blocks, the line vanishes. Should a line remain (20) ___, however, new blocks will build up on top of it. Once the screen's full, the game's over. Part of the (21) ___ is that anyone can play Tetris. Scientists also believe that the game's (22) ___ is due to the fact that it makes our brains work with greater (23) ___. The more we play, the easier it becomes, so we carry on, (24) ___ trying to beat our last high score.

example.explanation: Cần tính từ mang nghĩa 'không giới hạn'.

questions.explanation: Collocation: 'be under the impression that...' (có cảm tưởng rằng). Lỗi sai phổ biến: học sinh hay điền tính từ 'IMPRESSIVE'.
Cần danh từ sau mạo từ 'the'. Cụm 'further from the truth' (càng không phải là sự thật).
Cần danh từ: 'a variety of' (đa dạng). VARY -> VARIETY.
Cần tính từ trái nghĩa (nếu dòng đó vẫn chưa hoàn thiện). COMPLETE -> INCOMPLETE.
Cần danh từ sau 'the'. APPEALING (adj) -> APPEAL (n: sự hấp dẫn).
Cần danh từ sự phổ biến sau sở hữu cách 'game\'s'. POPULAR -> POPULARITY.
Cần danh từ sau tính từ 'greater'. EFFICIENT -> EFFICIENCY (sự hiệu quả).
Cần trạng từ bổ nghĩa cho động từ 'trying'. CONTINUE -> CONTINUALLY (liên tục).

passages.title: Smartphones at school
passages.text: Some of the schools in my home town are really strict and students are not (0) ALLOWED to use their smartphones at school. Mine is different - there's a much more (17) ___ policy. In break times, it's (18) ___ to use our smartphones. But in lessons, it's the individual teacher's (19) ___ whether we can use them or not. For some pieces of work, like a timed writing task, they're completely (20) ___. Of course it's our (21) ___ to follow the rules, which we do. In some lessons, the teachers actively encourage us to use our phones when they think it'll be (22) ___ to us. There are lots of really good ways to use smartphones in class, and I'm in favour of these. One example is games, where we choose multiple-choice answers on our phones. I'm really (23) ___ so love doing those. Although it can be a bit (24) ___ when everyone just uses their phone instead of talking.

example.explanation: Bị động: be allowed to do sth.

questions.explanation: Cần tính từ đứng trước danh từ 'policy'. RELAX -> RELAXED (thoải mái).
Cần tính từ sau 'it\'s'. ACCEPT -> ACCEPTABLE (có thể chấp nhận được).
Cần danh từ sau sở hữu cách 'teacher\'s'. DECIDE -> DECISION (quyết định).
Cần tính từ (bị động) mang nghĩa cấm đoán. FORBID -> FORBIDDEN.
Cần danh từ sau 'our'. RESPONSIBLE -> RESPONSIBILITY (trách nhiệm).
Cần tính từ sau 'be'. BENEFIT -> BENEFICIAL (có lợi).
Cần tính từ miêu tả tính cách (người có tính cạnh tranh). COMPETE -> COMPETITIVE.
Cần tính từ mang nghĩa tiêu cực (chỉ cắm mặt vào điện thoại thay vì nói chuyện). SOCIAL -> ANTI-SOCIAL (phản xã hội/ngại giao tiếp).

passages.title: The future of food
passages.text: With the world population rising and (0) AVAILABILITY of food sources decreasing, what will we be eating in the future? Food science (17) ___ say that by 2050, we will be consuming less meat and more fish. And with food prices rising, another likely result is that more of us will reach the (18) ___ that becoming vegetarian is a good idea. Then there is the (19) ___ that we will eat more seaweed and insects, and perhaps even 'superfood' bars, which would be (20) ___ to the kinds of things astronauts eat in space. Today's technology could (21) ___ manufacturers to produce what may become known as 'functional' foods. These are foods that are (22) ___ designed for different sectors of (23) ___. This means that there will be foods adapted not only for babies, which we already see in supermarkets, but for teens, women, men and elderly people, too. Because the food will be (24) ___ engineered, we will also be healthier!

questions.explanation: Cần danh từ chỉ người số nhiều (các chuyên gia) làm chủ ngữ cho động từ 'say'. SPECIAL -> SPECIALISTS.
Collocation: 'reach the conclusion' (đi đến kết luận). CONCLUDE -> CONCLUSION.
Cần danh từ sau mạo từ 'the'. POSSIBLE -> POSSIBILITY (khả năng).
Cần tính từ đi với giới từ 'to'. SAME thì đi với 'as', SIMILAR đi với 'to'. SAME -> SIMILAR.
Cần động từ nguyên mẫu sau 'could'. ABLE -> ENABLE (cho phép).
Cần trạng từ bổ nghĩa cho động từ bị động 'designed'. INDIVIDUAL -> INDIVIDUALLY (được thiết kế riêng biệt).
Cần danh từ sau giới từ 'of'. SOCIAL -> SOCIETY (xã hội).
Cần trạng từ bổ nghĩa cho 'engineered'. Lỗi sai phổ biến: điền danh từ SCIENTIST hoặc tính từ SCIENTIFIC mà quên đổi sang trạng từ.

passages.title: Hope in a Ballet Shoe
passages.text: Hope in a Ballet Shoe is the (0) POWERFUL autobiography of ballet dancer Michaela DePrince. Michaela had a strict (17) ___ in Sierra Leone. There are several (18) ___ of her early days there which are particularly well-written. A turning point in her life was her (19) ___ by an American couple at the age of four, and her move with them to the USA. At first, she found that all the cultural (20) ___ were hard to get used to. But she grew to love her new home, and the book shows how love and (21) ___ can overcome these difficulties - and help people to achieve their goals. Michaela was absolutely (22) ___ to become a ballet dancer and practised for hours on end. Her family were there for her all the time, supporting her and eventually (23) ___ her to succeed. Michaela is now a world-famous ballerina with the Dutch National Ballet. I would certainly recommend Hope in a Ballet Shoe to anyone from the age of eleven upwards. It truly is a (24) ___ story.

questions.explanation: Cần danh từ sau tính từ 'strict'. BRING -> UPBRINGING (sự nuôi dưỡng).
Cần danh từ số nhiều sau 'several'. DESCRIBE -> DESCRIPTIONS (những lời miêu tả).
Cần danh từ (sự nhận nuôi). ADOPT -> ADOPTION.
Cần danh từ số nhiều (những sự khác biệt). DIFFERENT -> DIFFERENCES.
Cần danh từ song song với 'love'. Học sinh thường quên đổi thành danh từ mà giữ nguyên 'PATIENT' (tính từ/bệnh nhân).
Cần tính từ (mang tính quyết tâm). DETERMINE -> DETERMINED.
Cần động từ chia V-ing song song với 'supporting' (enabling) hoặc V-ed (enabled). Cả hai đều chấp nhận.
Cần tính từ mang nghĩa cảm động. MOVE -> MOVING.

passages.title: Peanut butter is good for you!
passages.text: With 'peanuts' and 'butter' in its name, peanut butter doesn't (0) EXACTLY sound good for us. Common sense tells us that neither food is a particularly healthy (17) ___, given their high fat content. The product's (18) ___, however, has led to research, and it's good news to learn that many food science (19) ___ say it isn't as bad for us as we might have thought. Peanut butter is (20) ___ versatile it can be eaten with everything from raw vegetable sticks to toast - but the fat it contains is also monounsaturated (the 'right' kind of fat), which is believed to be good for the heart. Peanut butter also contains a (21) ___ amount of protein, which contains important nutrients for growing kids. (22) ___ it contains iron, B vitamins and fibre, all of which are necessary for the (23) ___ of a healthy body. So, next time you're told to put the top back on the jar, you'll be able to offer an (24) ___ as to why just one more spoonful is good for you!

example.explanation: Cần trạng từ bổ nghĩa cho động từ sound.

questions.explanation: Cần danh từ sau tính từ 'healthy'. CHOOSE -> CHOICE (sự lựa chọn).
Cần danh từ sau sở hữu cách 'product\'s'. Lỗi sai phổ biến: nhầm với tính từ 'POPULOUS' (đông dân).
Cần danh từ chỉ người số nhiều làm chủ ngữ. SPECIAL -> SPECIALISTS (chuyên gia).
Cần trạng từ mang nghĩa chắc chắn/không còn nghi ngờ gì. DOUBT -> UNDOUBTEDLY.
Cần tính từ bổ nghĩa cho 'amount'. SUBSTANCE -> SUBSTANTIAL (đáng kể).
Cần trạng từ đứng đầu câu làm từ nối. ADD -> ADDITIONALLY (ngoài ra/hơn nữa).
Cần danh từ (sự duy trì). MAINTAIN -> MAINTENANCE.
Cần danh từ sau mạo từ 'an'. EXPLAIN -> EXPLANATION (lời giải thích).
